title: System Prompt
description:
Ongoing evolution of the agent system prompt. The prompt defines agent behavior across all projects — it's the most leveraged piece of text in the system.

## Principles
- For ALL projects, not just Matrix — no internal terminology (JSONL, daemon, provider loop)
- Agent doesn't know system internals — document message formats so agents can distinguish sources
- Failure model: only done(passed) and done(failed) — no interrupted/stuck visible to agents

## Recent Changes
- WHY requirement strengthened to MUST level
- Message format documentation (XML tags vs plain text)
- Message source response rules
- Data layout section added
- Internal terminology removed
- "Always resume first" → deliberate decision-making
- Last-minute report anti-pattern: "done() IS your final report"
- Generic examples replacing Matrix-specific ones

## Open Areas
- Prompt reorder: /tmp/prompt-reordered.txt generated with new chapter structure, user reviewing
- Depth encouragement: currently says "keep shallow 2-3 levels" — suppresses natural depth
- Draft task emphasis: "capture ideas immediately" philosophy
- Pinned agent patterns: how to instruct agents about domain owners
